title: The Padded Room
wikitext:
[[File:Federal Bureau of Extrauniversal Affairs (Logo).png|center|200px]]
This file is classified and/or contains confidential information. Any personnel who views this document without express permission from the Federal Bureau Of Extrauniversal Affairs will be punished accordingly. Handling, copies, or disposition is highly restricted; unless fitting with the applicable laws.
Threshold 001 is the primary objective of '''Project Deus Ex Machina'''. It consists of three main spaces:
* 001-1: Site-02, a looping non-euclidean threshold consisting of an office building, following the fundamental laws of physics, used to observe 001-2. Status: COMPELETED *001-2: A liminal space consisting of facility consisting of the necessary parts to create 001-3. Status: COMPLETED *001-3: A liminal space consisting of a single 5x5x5 meter box, maintained at 0.000000000000000000000000018 contexons. Will be used to access higher narratives that are pataphysically linked to our universe, by using the infinite narrative potential of a highly liminal space to contact entities in higher narratives via a vessel. Status: ONGOING
===Liminal Spaces=== What is liminality? In Lim-Physics, liminality is the state of an area where the narrative complexity (measured by contexons, which are a value of how connected to other narrative elements is a narrative element) is at very low levels, resulting in the area having near-infinite narrative potential. Many limspaces use infinite/looping space to maintain low conteon count, while others use antinarramemes, which are narrative elements that when introduced into a narrative, results in mutual destruction of the narrative. Liminal spaces could theoretically be used to create custom narratives on a equal level to our universal narrative, which could then be used to create a strong pataphysical link that could theoretically create a higher narrative than our universal narrative, escaping the control of higher entities. However, this endeavor would need assistance from entities in higher narratives, as they would need to assist in the process of creating a higher narrative.
===Experiment Logs === To generate the antinarramemes able to create a liminal space in such a small space, narrative tension will need to be harvested in order to generate the necessary narrative elements. Various liminal spaces will be generated to aid in this effort. To generate high tension narratives, it is important that personnel working on this project treat it with the utmost importance. * [[Level !|Experiment 1]]: A limspace consisting of a long hospital hallway. A narrative was generated, depicting Agent-752 (who had entered the liminal space before activation of the narrative) running from various entities. The following narrative had a unpredicted effect of activating whenever anyone entered the liminal space, as discovered by a recovery team looking for Agent-752's body. Research on how to reproduce this effect is ongoing. *Experiment 2: A limspace consisting of a single house. A narrative was generated, consisting of an entity asking anyone who enters the level what 9+10 equals. The narrative is much less dangerous to the person inside it than experiment 1, however much more narrative tension is generated due to the comedic tension and the widespread memetic dissemination of the narrative elements. *[[Level 18|Experiment 3]]: A limspace consisting of a copy of a childhood memory of the person entering it. Due to its properties, high tension narratives were naturally generated. <245 ENTRIES OMMITTED.> > No
SUFFICIENT NARRATIVE TENSION GENERATED. GENERATING ANTINARRAMEME...
You're all insane. Ever since you started feeding things into this limspace, there's been more anomalous phenomena happening in the site, even ''outside'' the site. There's been more reports of people going missing, more reports of strange thing happening. God damn it, I shouldn't have to come home just to find out that ''my fucking dishes are floating''. Anyways, that's beside the point. If my calculations are correct, the full implementation of this project has a 47% chance of irreparably damaging reality. That's not even "taking a risk" or "increasing narrative tension" at that point, it's fucking russian roulette. Take this as my official letter of resignation. Fuck. You. -Head Researcher, ██████ ████
Experiment: Full implementation of the project, at an decreased contexon level of 0.00000000000000000000000000000000067. The vessel used will be Head Researcher ██████ ████, who is instructed to deliver a message to higher narratives as a punishment for violence against administration after a conflict regarding their resignation. The vessel's head will be covered with a bag, and all personnel working on the transportation process will not be informed of their identity. Result:[DATA LOST]

The cat looked out on the ruined town. After a long time in that [[Transcendence|box]], it was finally free, but in a hollow husk of its former home, which they could barely remember. It wasn't worth being there, so they walked, eventually finding an exit to a [[Level 37.1|dark pool]]. The cat screamed for help. Nobody came. Determined, the cat continued wandering, the weight of the void muffling any sound that might have come out. The cat fell into the void.
